title: NBA Daily Rundown: July 22, 2026
subtitle: LeBron Watch Heats Up, Summer League Stars Shine

items.headline: LeBron James Free Agency Saga Intensifies Amidst Heat Buzz
items.summary: The NBA world is on high alert as LeBron James' free agency decision looms, with the Miami Heat emerging as a strong contender. Speculation reached a fever pitch after the Heat reportedly posted and quickly removed a video of a LeBron introductory press conference. This comes on the heels of Miami's blockbuster trade on June 22nd to acquire two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks, a move that significantly reshapes the Eastern Conference landscape. James is reportedly also considering the Cleveland Cavaliers and Philadelphia 76ers.

items.headline: Stephen Curry Makes History with Active Player Hall of Fame Exhibit
items.summary: Golden State Warriors superstar Stephen Curry is set to make history with a dedicated exhibit, 'Stephen Curry: Beyond the Arc,' opening at the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame this Friday. This marks the first time an active player has been honored with a feature experience in the museum's nearly 75-year history, celebrating the career of the two-time MVP and NBA's all-time leader in three-pointers.

items.headline: No NBA Games Played on July 21, 2026
items.summary: The NBA schedule for July 21, 2026, and July 22, 2026, shows no regular season or playoff games were scheduled as the league is currently in its offseason. Preseason games are expected to begin in October.

items.headline: Yaxel Lendeborg Crowned Summer League MVP
items.summary: Golden State Warriors' Yaxel Lendeborg, the No. 11 overall pick, earned Most Valuable Player honors at the 2026 NBA Summer League. He led the Warriors to the championship, delivering 21 points and 10 rebounds in the title game victory over the MEM Grizzlies. Throughout the Summer League, Lendeborg averaged 14.9 points, 6.8 rebounds, 4.0 assists, and 1.4 steals.

items.headline: Brayden Burries Named to All-Summer League First Team
items.summary: Milwaukee Bucks guard Brayden Burries, the 10th overall pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, was named to the 2026 NBA All-Summer League First Team. Burries averaged a team-high 23.5 points, 4.5 rebounds, and 4.3 assists in four games for the Bucks.

items.headline: Deni Avdija a Training Target for Bulls Rookie
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers All-Star Deni Avdija continues to be a player of interest around the league. Chicago Bulls rookie Matas Buzelis expressed a desire to train with Avdija to refine his rim-attacking skills. Avdija had an All-Star season, averaging 24.2 points, 6.9 rebounds, and 6.7 assists per game.

items.headline: Additional Rookie Standouts from NBA Summer League
items.summary: Several other rookies made an immediate impact in Summer League. Morez Johnson Jr. (DAL) averaged 18.5 points, 6.5 rebounds, 3.0 blocks, and 2.5 steals. Aday Mara (OKC) showcased versatility with 9.2 points, 7.0 rebounds, 3.2 assists, and 1.8 blocks. Cameron Carr (LAL) also impressed, averaging 18.0 points over five games.

items.headline: P.J. Washington to Lakers Reunion Hits Snag
items.summary: Reports suggest that Dallas Mavericks forward P.J. Washington's potential reunion with the Los Angeles Lakers has hit a significant roadblock. Despite earlier front-office inquiries, league insiders indicate there's no real trade infrastructure currently in place, cooling earlier speculation. Washington signed an $88.76 million extension with Dallas last September.

items.headline: Mario Hezonja Eyeing NBA Return, Linked to LeBron Pursuers
items.summary: Former No. 5 overall pick Mario Hezonja is reportedly planning an NBA comeback after not playing in the league since the 2019-20 season. Multiple NBA teams are showing interest in the Real Madrid forward, including some of the same franchises currently pursuing LeBron James.

items.headline: Latest Star Player Injury Updates
items.summary: Key injury updates around the league include Utah Jazz's Walker Kessler, who is out for the remainder of the 2025-26 season after undergoing left shoulder surgery for a torn labrum. Ousmane Gueye (ATL) is expected to be re-evaluated in 3-4 months following foot surgery. Santi Aldama (MEM) is also out for the season due to a right knee injury requiring arthroscopic surgery.

items.headline: Early Look: 2026-27 NBA Season Power Rankings
items.summary: With the NBA offseason settling, early power rankings for the 2026-27 season are emerging. The Oklahoma City Thunder lead the pack, projected for a 64-18 record after a 64-18 finish last season. The San Antonio Spurs are ranked second (projected 57-22), followed by the Toronto Raptors (projected 54-28). The New York Knicks, last season's champions, are also highly rated.
